Fledglings

Author

Mara Bodenmann

Published

April 24, 2026

Research question:
How do environmental stressors and resource availability influence reproductive success?

1 Prepare Session

Import libraries

Code 
  library(pastecs)
  library(ggpubr); theme_set(theme_bw())
  library(GGally)
  library(performance)
  library(MuMIn)
  library(ggeffects)
  library(tidyverse)

  library(knitr)

Import the data

Code 
clean <- read_csv("data/processed/clean.CSV")

Modify the data

Code 
#deal with NA--> remove the row with this NA
fledglings<- clean%>%
  select(n_fledglings, insect_abundance, env_noise)%>%
  na.omit() #removes rows with na, can specify column: filter(!is.na(column_name))

Here the flags and the row with the NA was removed.

2 Explore the data

2.1 Numerically

Code 
numexp<-stat.desc(fledglings,norm = T)

kable(numexp, digits=1)

2.2 Graphically

Code 
expgraph<-ggarrange(
  ggplot(fledglings, aes(x=insect_abundance,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black")+
    labs(x=expression("Insect abundance in (g/m" ^2*")"), y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest", title="A"),
  ggplot(fledglings, aes(x=env_noise,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black")+
    labs(x="Environmental ambient noise (db)", y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest", title="B")
)
expgraph

3 Fit the models

Fitting different models to see which one is most suitable for the data

Code 
mod1<-glm(n_fledglings~ 1, family= "poisson", data=fledglings)
mod2<-glm(n_fledglings~ insect_abundance + env_noise , family= "poisson", data=fledglings)
mod3<-glm(n_fledglings~ insect_abundance * env_noise , family= "poisson", data=fledglings)

3.1 Evaluate the models

Choose which one fits the best.

Code 
anv.models<-anova(mod1,mod2,mod3)
kable(anv.models)

The model I choose is model 2 where there is no interaction between the insect abundance and the environment noise. Because with the interaction, the model was not significantly better than the one without the interaction.

4 Validate the model

Validate whether I should proceed with the model

First I have to check for overdispersion because it is a glm!

Code 
check_overdispersion(mod2)
# Overdispersion test

       dispersion ratio = 0.028
  Pearson's Chi-Squared = 1.020
                p-value =     1

There was no overdispersion detected, we can continue with checking the model.

Code 
check_model(mod2)

This also looks alright, I can continue with interpreting the model

5 Interpreting the model

Code 
#coefficients
coef.table<-round(summary(mod2)$coefficients,3)

#chi^2 of my model
null.chi<-anova(mod1,mod2)

#get r squared adjusted
r.2<- round(attributes(r.squaredLR(mod2))$adj.r.squared, 3)

#prepare the predictions plot
pred.mod<-predict_response(mod2)

#make the plot
pred.plot <-ggarrange(
  ggplot(pred.mod$insect_abundance, aes(x,predicted))+
    geom_point(data=fledglings,aes(x=insect_abundance,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin= conf.low, ymax= conf.high), alpha=0.2)+
    geom_line()+
    labs(x=expression("Insect abundance in (g/m" ^2*")"), y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest", title = "A"),
  ggplot(pred.mod$env_noise, aes(x,predicted))+
    geom_point(data=fledglings,aes(x=env_noise,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin= conf.low, ymax= conf.high), alpha=0.2)+
    geom_line()+
    labs(x="Envronmental ambient noise (db)", y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest", title = "B"),
  ncol=2,nrow=1
  
)
                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept)         0.933      1.386   0.673    0.501
insect_abundance    0.081      0.038   2.115    0.034
env_noise          -0.005      0.018  -0.259    0.796

A poisson Generalized Linear Model (R2= 0.291 , \(\chi^2_{( 2 , 36 )}\)= 13.067 , p0.001454; Table 3) performed significantly better than a null-model.

The model generated moderate compelling support for the hypothesized effect of insect abundance on successfully raised fledglings per nest (\(\beta_{Insect Abundance}\)= 0.081 , SE= 0.038 , z= 2.115, p= 0.034) .

However no compelling support was generated by the model for the effect of environmental ambient noise on successfully raised fledglings per nest (\(\beta_{Environmental AmbientNoise}\)= -0.005 , SE= 0.018 , z= -0.259, p= 0.796) Thess findings is corroborated by Figure 3 , in which we see that, as the insect abundance increases the number of fledglings increases.

6 Store session information

Code 
  version<- sessionInfo()
